hard.next_step: Jul 02, 2026 (due Jul 02, 2026): Check ToDo in activities 6/24/26 Mtg with Katie to confirm Zycus is VOC going into final demo. iValua in only because they were shortlisted last initiative and Oracle at IT's insistence. RISK: some customization needed: With ROnit and Bikash. IT is a wild card, but no one is happy with Oracle as a partner. The VP of SC - the executive Sponsor wants a vendor - led implementation. SOmething Oracle and iValua won't do. 6/23/2026 Meeting to discuss final requirements match and map final steps, including final demo 6/22/26 Submitted new requirements assessment 6/18/26 RH New timeline - final RFP submission with new requirements - 7/18/26 Ronit to finalize new Direct components (confirm greatly minimized focus on direct) Reference request for 3 implementation levels: Smooth - some issues - problematic -
hard.ais_why: On the 73-minute 2/2/2026 demo, Yufeng Chen drove the AI conversation himself (asked about agent executability, AI R&D spend, ANA negotiation guardrails) and committed to share 4,400 lines of Amazon Business transaction data under NDA for a classification-accuracy demo. Buyer-funded, buyer-initiated POC behavior; he wants deeper insight into algorithms and control mechanisms.

forecast_read.recommended_forecast: Upside Key Deal, with the close date reset to September 2026
forecast_read.reason: This is a date problem on a healthy, engaged deal, not a deal problem: the buyer's own final RFP submission deadline is 18 Jul 2026, scored demos run 13-17 Jul, the economic buyer has not joined a call, and no MSA/SOW/legal work exists, so a 24 Jul 2026 close leaves six days for scoring, executive review, pricing sign-off, and a paper process that has not begun.
forecast_read.math: 15 days to close (2026-07-09 to 2026-07-24). In Shortlisted since 15 Mar 2026 (116 days), but the evaluation was re-scoped in May 2026 around a 217-line SOW and is pacing to the buyer's process clock: Zycus RFP response submitted 8 Jul, four scored demo sessions 13-17 Jul, priced final submission due 18 Jul. Close date has already slipped twice (30 Mar to 15 May on 15 Mar; 26 Jun to 24 Jul on 18 Jun). Post-submission scoring, executive review and contracting realistically land the close in September 2026.

metrics.narrative: Confirmed pain is operational, not yet fully quantified: Katie Booth and Yufeng Chen described an urgent need for sourcing and supplier-information modules with performance and dashboards 'increasingly difficult to manage' (Avoma call 10 Mar 2026), and Dan Chao gave a concrete efficiency anchor on the 24 Jun 2026 call: 'last week, it took me an hour to put a package together. There's 14 drawings.' Zycus proved classification value live by processing S&C's 4,400 lines of Amazon spend data during the 2 Feb 2026 AI session, but no buyer-stated dollar savings or cycle-time figure has been captured on any read call. Quantifying the business case before the priced RFP lands with leadership is the open move.

economic_buyer.narrative: Jim Boss (VP Global Operational Excellence) is the SF-recorded Executive Sponsor/Decision Maker and David Single (VP Finance) a named Evaluator, but neither has appeared as a speaker on any read call. The Next Step (24 Jun 2026) names 'the VP of SC - the executive Sponsor' as actively wanting a vendor-led implementation that Oracle and Ivalua reportedly won't do, consistent with the January 2026 read that the initiative is driven by Kara Weiner, VP of Global Supply Chain. Leadership is demonstrably involved indirectly: Yufeng Chen asked for the priced RFP response 'as soon as you can... so I can plug that number in leadership's mind' (Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). Direct Zycus face time with Kara Weiner or Jim Boss remains the priority gap before scoring concludes.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Jim Boss, David Single, Kara Weiner)

decision_criteria.narrative: S&C runs a 217-line RFP/SOW tabulated by functional category with a common SME scorecard: 'The deliverable... reads exactly how it does in the SOW. It's ordered 12345... same priority, same functional category, and scoring' with drop-down scores per deliverable (Katie Booth, Avoma call 30 Jun 2026). Must-have vs nice-to-have guidance was put in writing on 25 Jun 2026 (VMI gaps largely tolerable, 3DX/PLM artifact access foundational), and Yufeng Chen confirmed direct-material eProcurement is 'very less likely' in phase one, scored 'with and without' (Avoma calls 24 and 30 Jun 2026). Zycus knows exactly what is being scored and in what order.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence maps the process: Dan Chao (Senior Program Manager) drives demo logistics across roughly 35 SMEs with a different audience per module; Katie Booth manages evaluation mechanics and scoring; Yufeng Chen adjudicates scope and fit in real time. The concrete sequence is fixed: scored demo sessions 13-17 Jul 2026, priced final RFP submission by 18 Jul 2026, then SME scorecards roll up ('they provide us the scorecard as soon as they are finished scoring', Katie Booth, 30 Jun 2026). Bob King's IT angle remains the wildcard: an IT deep-dive with S&C's IT lead (named as Pete on the 24 Jun 2026 call) is anticipated as part of the RFP but not yet scheduled.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA/SOW/legal milestone has surfaced on any read call and Harman Toor (Commercial Corporate Counsel, a named contact role) has never appeared as a speaker. With the final RFP submission due 18 Jul 2026 and the recorded close at 24 Jul 2026, there is no visible contracting runway. Ronit stated on 24 Jun 2026 he 'will be working on an MSA and SOW... with Rick' if selected, but nothing has started. Getting a documented paper-process plan (whose paper, InfoSec timing, counsel engagement) agreed right after demo scoring is what would make any close date credible.

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed and owned by the Global Sourcing/Supply Chain team: 'ongoing indecision and conflict regarding responses and actions' with bidders (Avoma call 14 Jan 2026), urgent module and dashboard needs (Avoma call 10 Mar 2026), and Yufeng Chen's own framing that S&C is far behind on digital transformation (Avoma call 3 Jun 2026). The 24 Jun 2026 call added a specific operational pain: suppliers receiving POs for a revision without ever receiving the drawing, and sourcing managers spending an hour assembling a 14-drawing artifact package by hand (Dan Chao). The 3DX/PLM artifact grab is 'a foundational need' whose fix 'would immediately enhance the experience for everyone using the platform' (Dan Chao, 24 Jun 2026).

champion.narrative: Yufeng Chen (Global Sourcing Manager) is a strong, active champion: he talked Zycus out of withdrawing over direct-material gaps (3 Jun 2026), told the team 'You guys are still in a very good shape' and 'none of those will be treated as a deal breaker' (24 Jun 2026), and confirmed Zycus as vendor of choice going into the final demo (Katie meeting, 24 Jun 2026, per Next Step). Katie Booth (Global Indirect Sourcing Manager - IT) operates as an operational co-champion, coaching Zycus on demo mechanics ('if you think you need an hour, consider an hour and a half', 30 Jun 2026) and chasing references herself on 29 Jun 2026. Both have direct access to the evaluation machinery; neither shows any cooling.

competition.narrative: Zycus is the buyer-stated vendor of choice as of 24 Jun 2026, but the bake-off is live: rival demo sessions are running in parallel ('we're finding your competitors are spending avg 1 hour per section', Dan Chao email, 6 Jul 2026). Oracle Procurement Cloud is the strongest current threat, in 'at IT's insistence' via CIO Bob King even though 'no one is happy with Oracle as a partner' and Yufeng concedes Oracle is strong on direct-material procurement while being hard to configure (30 Jun 2026). Ivalua is a low-threat holdover retained only because it was shortlisted in a prior initiative. Coupa exists only in the SF competitor field with no live evidence this run. The wedge is the executive sponsor's vendor-led implementation requirement, which Oracle and Ivalua reportedly won't meet, plus Zycus's AI depth and candor the buyer explicitly values ('you guys have been extremely honest and candid with us... we view that as a positive', Yufeng Chen, 30 Jun 2026).

critical_signals.text: Zycus goes into the 13-17 Jul scored demo week as the named vendor of choice, but rivals are demoing in parallel and Oracle stays in the race through the CIO's insistence even though the buying team says no one is happy with Oracle as a partner.

On 30 Jun Zycus confirmed it will build the 3DX/PLM capability through an app and already has the supplier-side VMI inventory-visibility capability, and Yufeng Chen immediately asked for the priced response so he can put the dollar figure in leadership's mind.
The full RFP response went to S&C on 8 Jul via the shared workspace, and the priced final submission with the new requirements is due 18 Jul, six days before the recorded close date.
Katie Booth scoped CLM down to integration only on 24 Jun: S&C keeps DocuSign CLM and wants visibility into contracted terms from Zycus, not contract authoring or redlining.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely hot, buyer-driven evaluation: Zycus is the stated vendor of choice with a strong champion pair, the RFP response landed 8 Jul, and four scored demo sessions sit on S&C calendars for 13-17 Jul. But the deal is still mid-scoring in a live bake-off, the economic buyer has never joined a call, no paper process exists, and the 24 Jul close date cannot survive an 18 Jul final-submission deadline.

win_position.text: The buyer confirmed Zycus as vendor of choice going into the final demo in the 24 Jun 2026 meeting with Katie Booth, with Ivalua held over only from a prior shortlist and Oracle in at IT's insistence while no one on the buying team is happy with Oracle as a partner.

Yufeng Chen and Katie Booth actively pull Zycus forward: Yufeng blocked a Zycus withdrawal on 3 Jun 2026, called the direct-material gaps 'not a deal breaker' on 24 Jun, and Katie chased references and coached demo mechanics through 30 Jun.
The executive sponsor (VP of Supply Chain) wants a vendor-led implementation that Oracle and Ivalua reportedly won't do, a structural differentiator recorded in the 24 Jun 2026 Next Step.
No award exists yet: SME scorecards only roll up after the 13-17 Jul demo sessions, and competitors are running their own sessions in parallel per Dan Chao's 6 Jul email.
Jim Boss and Kara Weiner have not appeared on a single call across the entire evaluation; leadership will see the Zycus number second-hand through Yufeng Chen.

deal_momentum.text: Zycus submitted the full RFP response on 8 Jul 2026 via the shared workspace, on the buyer's reset 18 Jul timeline.
The buyer is driving cadence: Dan Chao locked demo dates within 48 hours (6-8 Jul), split section 3 into five audience-specific sessions, and Katie Booth chased Zycus for references on 29 Jun.
Four buyer-accepted scored demo sessions are on S&C calendars for 13-17 Jul (Sourcing RFx, Supplier Info Management, Spend Analysis, Indirect eProcurement).
The close date has slipped twice (15 Mar and 18 Jun 2026, now 24 Jul) and the buyer's own 18 Jul submission deadline guarantees a third slip without a reset.

customer_commitment.text: Yufeng Chen asked for the priced RFP response 'as soon as you can' on 30 Jun so he can plug the dollar figure into leadership's thinking, and S&C requested reference discussions across three implementation-quality levels on 17 Jun.
S&C is investing roughly 35 SMEs across module-specific demo audiences with a formal drop-down scorecard, real structural skin in the game.
Commitment so far is evaluation commitment, not purchase commitment: no mutual close plan, no paper started, and budget sign-off authority (Jim Boss / Kara Weiner) unengaged.

deal_risk.text: Live multi-vendor fight at scoring time: competitors are demoing in parallel and Oracle retains a CIO-level internal advocate in Bob King.

The 3DX/PLM connector has never been built by Zycus ('this would be the first time', 24 Jun 2026) and cannot be demonstrated, only wireframed, while at least one rival reportedly has it out of the box.
The 24 Jul close date is not achievable against the 18 Jul final submission; without a proactive reset, the deal shows a third consecutive slip in the CRM.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced: Katie Booth confirmed Zycus is the vendor of choice going into the final demo (meeting, 24 Jun 2026, per Next Step); Yufeng Chen told Zycus 'You guys are still in a very good shape' and that no identified gap 'will be treated as a deal breaker' (Avoma call 24 Jun 2026), after blocking a Zycus withdrawal on 3 Jun 2026.

business_case.evidence: Operational pain is confirmed and specific (hour-long manual artifact packages, 14 drawings; suppliers missing drawing revisions; dashboards unmanageable) but no buyer-stated dollar savings or cycle-time target has been captured; Yufeng wants the Zycus price to take to leadership (30 Jun 2026), so the value case is still Zycus's to quantify.

deal_movement.summary: The evaluation shifted from requirements clarification into execution: Zycus's RFP response landed 8 Jul, demo week 13-17 Jul is locked with four scored sessions, and the amount grew 275k to 340k on 26 Jun, while the close date slipped a second time to 24 Jul on 18 Jun.

items.change: Zycus submitted the full RFP response to S&C via the shared workspace (Showpad shared space created and S&C invited)

Demo week locked: four scored sessions on S&C calendars, 13 Jul Sourcing RFx, 14 Jul Supplier Info Management, 16 Jul Spend Analysis, 17 Jul Indirect eProcurement; Dan Chao split section 3 into five audience-specific sessions
Amount raised from 275,000 to 340,000 by Richard Hunsinger
Close date pushed from 26 Jun to 24 Jul 2026, aligned to the new final RFP submission timeline of 18 Jul
Buyer delivered written guidance marking VMI gaps as tolerable and 3DX/PLM artifact access as the priority (Dan Chao email)

day_summary.overall: A heavy execution day on both sides. Zycus submitted the full RFP response to S&C through a new shared workspace with Yufeng Chen, Katie Booth and Dan Chao invited. Dan Chao worked the demo agenda in a live back-and-forth, splitting the section-3 content into five sessions because each area has a different SME audience, and confirmed he is securing dates against the 13-17 Jul demo week. One wrinkle: the Supplier Info Management demo slot collided with a pre-existing S&C meeting, which both sides worked through on the same thread.

items.name: RFP response submitted
items.summary: Zycus delivered the RFP response to Yufeng Chen, Dan Chao and Katie Booth via a shared file workspace set up for the S&C response.

items.name: Demo agenda finalized with Dan Chao
items.summary: Dan Chao asked to break the section-3 block into five audience-specific sessions and started securing dates for demo week; Zycus confirmed 90-minute sessions with room for two hours.

items.name: Supplier Info Management demo conflict
items.summary: The SIM demo invitation hit a conflict with a key S&C meeting; the thread confirmed the session is still expected and the timing is being resolved.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the buyer-stated vendor of choice as of 24 Jun 2026, entering a live scored bake-off: rival demo sessions are running in parallel (Dan Chao, 6 Jul 2026). Oracle Procurement Cloud is the strongest current threat, carried not by demo merit but by CIO Bob King's internal push, against a buying team that says no one is happy with Oracle as a partner; Oracle's real strength is direct-material procurement, which the buyer has largely scoped out of phase one. Ivalua is a low-threat holdover from a prior shortlist. Coupa appears only in the Salesforce competitor field with no live evidence. DocuSign CLM is a retained incumbent that scopes CLM down to an integration requirement rather than a competitive fight.

competitors.name: Oracle Procurement Cloud
competitors.quote: iValua in only because they were shortlisted last initiative and Oracle at IT's insistence... IT is a wild card, but no one is happy with Oracle as a partner.
competitors.source: SF Next_Step__c 24 Jun 2026; Avoma call 30 Jun 2026 (Yufeng: Oracle 'on the working material, the procurement side is as strong as it is... they have their own issues too')
competitors.how_we_win: Keep the executive sponsor's vendor-led implementation requirement front and center, something Oracle reportedly won't do, and get the IT deep-dive with S&C's IT lead scheduled to neutralize Bob King's internal advocacy; direct-material procurement, Oracle's strength, is already scoped out of phase one.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.quote: iValua in only because they were shortlisted last initiative.
competitors.how_we_win: No Ivalua-specific objection has surfaced on any call; hold the scorecard lead through demo week and the vendor-led implementation wedge applies to Ivalua as well.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c; Avoma call 2 Feb 2026 (network comparison question only)
competitors.how_we_win: No active Coupa evaluation evidenced; no action unless it resurfaces.

competitors.name: DocuSign CLM
competitors.quote: We don't need any of that because we have Docusign CLM. So the integration piece to Docusign CLM is what we're looking for.
competitors.how_we_win: Not a displacement fight: demonstrate clean integration and contract-data visibility into DocuSign CLM in the demo sessions rather than positioning iContract as a replacement.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: S&C scores against a 217-line SOW with per-module SME audiences. Zycus self-assesses roughly 85 percent coverage, with the deviations concentrated in direct-material eProcurement that the buyer has largely scoped out of phase one; the 3DX/PLM artifact grab is the one foundational ask Zycus must develop rather than demonstrate.

best_practice_check.summary: The evaluation machinery is being run well; the gaps are above it. The three levers that decide this deal now are executive access before scoring concludes, neutralizing Oracle's CIO-side advocacy through the IT deep-dive, and building the contracting runway a 24 Jul close pretends already exists.
flags: Executive access: Jim Boss and Kara Weiner have never joined a call in an 18-month-old deal now days from scoring; have Yufeng Chen broker a Zycus exec session with the VP of Supply Chain around the priced submission rather than letting leadership see only a number.

Oracle neutralization: Bob King's IT insistence keeps Oracle alive and the anticipated IT deep-dive with S&C's IT lead is still unscheduled; get it on the calendar during demo week so IT objections surface while Zycus is in the room.

Close-date and paper runway: final submission lands 18 Jul against a 24 Jul close with counsel (Harman Toor) unengaged; co-author a post-scoring plan (scoring, exec review, MSA/SOW, InfoSec) with Katie Booth and reset the close to a date the buyer's own process supports.

No cooling signal on any channel through 8 Jul 2026.

Merlin is in the product set and the buyer's own words credit Zycus's AI as a differentiator versus Oracle.

confidence_signals.summary: No cooling anywhere: the buyer initiates threads, responds within hours, chases Zycus for references and demo dates, splits sessions to fit more SMEs in, and the champion asks for pricing to socialize with leadership. Every confidence signal through 8 Jul 2026 points at a buyer running hard toward a decision.

Dan Chao turned demo scheduling around within 48 hours (6-8 Jul 2026) and expanded section 3 into five sessions to fit distinct SME audiences.
Yufeng Chen asked for the priced response 'as soon as you can' to put the number in leadership's mind (30 Jun 2026).
Roughly 35 SMEs committed to scored demo sessions the week of 13-17 Jul 2026.
Buyer told Zycus it is 'still in a very good shape' and the identified gaps are not deal breakers (Yufeng Chen, 24 Jun 2026).

items.expected_effect: Closes the one overdue buyer ask before scoring starts and removes a soft credibility drag against rivals already mid-demo.

items.action: Confirm the rebooked Supplier Info Management slot and the five section-3 session dates with Dan Chao.
items.trigger: SIM demo invite conflict and section-3 split raised by Dan Chao
items.expected_effect: Protects full scorecard coverage across all SME audiences in the 13-17 Jul window.

items.action: Submit the priced final RFP with the new requirements by the buyer's 18 Jul deadline.
items.trigger: Yufeng Chen wants the dollar figure to put in leadership's mind; new timeline set 18 Jun
items.expected_effect: Puts the Zycus number in front of the VP-level sponsors at the moment demo scores land, converting vendor-of-choice standing into a commercial decision.

items.action: Run the Hemant-led PLM/3DX wireframe session for S&C's direct team during demo week.
items.trigger: Zycus committed wireframes and Hemant's app-dev team on the 30 Jun call; 3DX artifact access is the buyer's foundational ask
items.expected_effect: Turns the biggest capability exposure into a credible build plan while direct-scope evaluators are scoring.

items.action: Book the IT deep-dive with S&C's IT lead to surface and answer IT's Oracle-leaning objections.
items.trigger: Next Step flags 'IT is a wild card' with Oracle in at IT's insistence; Dan Chao anticipated an IT deep-dive as part of the RFP
items.expected_effect: Neutralizes CIO Bob King's internal advocacy for Oracle before scorecards consolidate.

items.action: Have Yufeng Chen broker a Zycus executive session with Kara Weiner to align on vendor-led implementation and co-author the post-scoring close plan.
items.trigger: Priced submission and demo scoring complete; EB has never met Zycus directly
items.expected_effect: Converts indirect executive sponsorship into direct engagement, opens the paper process with counsel, and resets the close date to one the buyer's process supports.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: 120d activities + 365d field history read in full (Next Step, Next Step History via Next_Step__c journal, completed Tasks with Descriptions, Events, contact roles)
gaps: Jim Boss and Kara Weiner (executive tier) have never appeared as speakers on any read call, so executive sentiment is inferred from the champion's reporting, not observed.
The 24 Jun 'S&C Zycus Connect' and 8 Jun 'requirements review' transcripts are partial; facts from their unread portions are treated as unknown, not absent.
No buyer-stated dollar value case exists anywhere in the evidence; this is a genuine knowledge gap, not a field-hygiene gap.

drivers: ✅ Buyer confirmed Zycus as vendor of choice going into the final demo (Katie Booth meeting, 24 Jun 2026), with Ivalua held over only from a prior shortlist and Oracle in at IT's insistence while 'no one is happy with Oracle as a partner.'

✅ Strong, active champion pair: Yufeng Chen blocked a Zycus withdrawal on 3 Jun, declared the gaps non-deal-breakers on 24 Jun, and is carrying the Zycus price to leadership; Katie Booth chases Zycus deliverables herself (references, 29 Jun).

✅ The evaluation is fully live and buyer-funded: RFP response submitted 8 Jul, four scored demo sessions on ~35 SME calendars for 13-17 Jul, and the executive sponsor's vendor-led implementation requirement is a structural wedge Oracle and Ivalua reportedly won't match.

⚠️ Still mid-scoring in a live bake-off: competitors are running their own demo sessions (Dan Chao, 6 Jul) and no award exists until SME scorecards consolidate after 17 Jul. ► Run demo week to the buyer's SOW scoring order and land the priced submission by 18 Jul.

⚠️ The economic buyer is untouched: Jim Boss and Kara Weiner have never joined a call, so leadership will judge Zycus from a number and a champion's summary. ► Have Yufeng Chen broker an executive session with Kara Weiner immediately after scoring.

⚠️ Zero contracting runway against a 24 Jul close: final submission is due 18 Jul, no MSA/SOW exists, and counsel (Harman Toor) has never been engaged. ► Co-author the post-scoring close plan and reset the date to September.
✅ Zycus submitted the full RFP response on 8 Jul via the shared workspace, on the buyer's reset 18 Jul timeline, with Yufeng Chen, Katie Booth and Dan Chao invited into the space the same day.
✅ The buyer is driving the cadence: Dan Chao locked demo dates within 48 hours (6-8 Jul), split section 3 into five audience-specific sessions, and Katie Booth chased Zycus for references on 29 Jun.
✅ Four buyer-accepted scored demo sessions sit on S&C calendars for 13-17 Jul (Sourcing RFx, Supplier Info Management, Spend Analysis, Indirect eProcurement).
✅ Commercial topics are entering: Yufeng Chen asked for the priced response 'as soon as you can' to put the dollar in leadership's mind (30 Jun), and the amount rose 275k to 340k on 26 Jun.

⚠️ The close date has been pushed twice (15 Mar and 18 Jun, now 24 Jul) and the buyer's own 18 Jul submission deadline guarantees another slip. ► Reset the close to a post-scoring September date with a mutual plan.
⚠️ The Supplier Info Management demo slot hit a buyer calendar conflict on 8 Jul. ► Confirm the rebooked SIM session before demo week opens.

Configure the platform and run demo week to the SOW scoring order with distinct SME audiences per module, including the DocuSign CLM integration coverage

best_practices.item: Broker direct executive engagement with Kara Weiner (and Jim Boss) via Yufeng Chen around the priced submission, before scorecards consolidate
Neutralize Oracle's CIO-side advocacy: use the IT deep-dive to surface Bob King's objections while Zycus is in the room, anchored on the vendor-led implementation wedge
Open the paper-process conversation (whose paper, MSA/SOW, InfoSec timing, Harman Toor engagement) immediately after demo scoring; this is a heavy step needing lead time
best_practices.source: Avoma call 24 Jun 2026; no legal track exists

best_practices.item: Quantify the business case (hours per RFx package, drawing-revision errors, dashboard effort) so leadership sees ROI beside the price, not just a number
best_practices.source: Avoma calls 24 Jun and 30 Jun 2026; no buyer-stated dollar case captured

sum.headline: S&C received Zycus's full RFP response on 8 Jul through a new shared workspace, keeping the evaluation on the buyer's 18 Jul final-submission track.
supporting: Dan Chao locked in the demo plan the same day, splitting section 3 into five audience-specific sessions and securing dates against the 13-17 Jul demo week.
One slip to manage: the Supplier Info Management demo slot collided with a key S&C meeting and needs a rebooked time before demo week opens.
